selected_default_policy.residual_formulation: encode_sdir on per-family weight delta (W_ref - W_low)

alternative_policy.description: 1 cos failure (best), 1 severe (same as k=1.0), 0 MAE failures (best)
alternative_policy.when_to_consider: if MAE failures are specifically unacceptable and severe count is tolerable

accepted_outlier.cause: per-family weight residual directionally misaligned with actual correction needed for this specific activation
accepted_outlier.k_sensitivity: no k > 0 fixes it without creating worse tradeoffs
accepted_outlier.output_residual_fix: confirmed by oracle — output residual fixes it, but requires activation-specific Y_ref at runtime
accepted_outlier.static_path_fix: none found — only k=0 eliminates severe, at cost of no residual benefit

reason_output_residual_not_selected: Activation-specific: requires Y_ref at runtime to compute R_y = Y_ref - Y_low
Cannot be precomputed statically: Y_ref depends on the input activation X
Not a static-deployable architecture: would need runtime oracle or reference activation source
Fix exists but not statically implementable under current architecture
Marked as future dynamic-architecture research direction
future_work: Dynamic/output residual architecture: requires runtime Y_ref oracle or learned predictor
Learned correction: trained model predicts correct residual direction per activation
Larger model testing: Qwen2.5-0.5B only; 1B/3B/7B+ behavior unknown
Eventual llama.cpp integration: only after more proof and outlier resolution or explicit acceptance
forbidden_claims: no full-model runtime memory savings claim
no claim beyond standalone tensor harness
